Title: Picking the right domain
Summary: Quick decision tree: which surface should you author your thing on?
Body:
## The decision tree

Ask, in order:

1. Is it a robot or person the player can talk to or kill? It is an [NPC](/help/game-domains/npcs).
2. Is it something the player can pick up, equip, or hand in? It is an [Item](/help/game-domains/items).
3. Is it a structured mission with objectives and rewards? It is a [Quest](/help/game-domains/quests).
4. Is it an invisible reputation effect? It is a [Karma impact](/help/game-domains/karma).
5. Is it a player facing reputation group? It is a [Faction](/help/game-domains/factions).
6. Is it a branching conversation? It is a [Dialog sequence](/help/dialogs-and-the-graph/sequences).
7. Is it the small line a robot says when the player walks up? It is a [Balloon](/help/balloons/what-balloons-are).

If none of those fit, you probably want the [Game codex](/help/codex/why-codex-exists) instead. The codex is for project specific concepts (Hazards, Locations, Vehicles) that have no Godot resource counterpart yet.

## What is shared

Every game domain follows the same surface conventions:

- A list page with cards and rows view modes.
- An edit page reachable by clicking a card or a row.
- A back link on the edit page that returns to the right list with the right filter still active.
- Save flows JSON first, then `.tres`, with the activity surfaced in the [Saves tab](/diagnostics/saves).
- Catalog autocomplete: fields that reference another entity (NPC id, Item slug, Quest id) get a datalist dropdown so you do not have to remember exact ids.
